title: SSC CGL T-I Reasoning Section Test

questions.question: 18 is related to 107 following a certain logic. Following the same logic, 21 is related to125. To which of the given options is 14 related, following the same logic? (Note: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking downthe numbers into their constituent digits. E.g., 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.)
questions.explanation: Information Given: 18 → 107 21 → 125 Find relation for 14. Logic: ​Number × 6, then minus 1. ​18 → 107 18 × 6 − 1 = 108 − 1 = 107 21 → 125 21 × 6 − 1 = 126 − 1 = 125 ✔ Now for 14: 14 × 6 − 1 = 84 − 1 = 83 Final Answer: 83 Final Correct Option: D

questions.question: In a certain code language, ‘SKATE’ is coded as ‘51324’ and ‘ASKEW’ is coded as ‘47315’. How is ‘W’ coded in that language?
questions.explanation: Given: In a certain code language, ‘SKATE’ is coded as ‘51324’ and ‘ASKEW’ is coded as ‘47315’. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/acc5794a-8295-448d-ba37-90edeb12c9ff.png) So, the code of W is 7. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: Statement: 1. All pens are pencils. 2. Some pencils are red. 3. No red is green. Conclusions: I. Some pens are not green. II. All greens are pencils. ​​
questions.explanation: Statement: 1. All pens are pencils. 2. Some pencils are red. 3. No red is green. From the given statements possible Venn diagram will be. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/8b0a66d0-40e1-4c07-93d1-a6882fd14b7e.png) Conclusions: I. Some pens are not green. (False, but we are not given that those red pencils are pens. So we cannot say that any pen is definitely not green.). II. All greens are pencils. (False, there is no relation between greens and pencils). So, ​Neither I nor II follows. Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/d31d6e77-b258-47f4-8926-b238ee713727.png)
questions.explanation: Given ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/00cde93e-c7fc-47f1-978a-29ad6c2c6a66.png) After interchanging 4 and 5 ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/fd1b959f-61f8-4bed-884b-7065aa0d6a6c.png) Logic: Using the red arrows as a guide, we can confirm how the cube folds and how the symbols and arrows align correctly in the final cube. The complete series is shown below: Thus, the correct option is (d)

questions.question: A number is divisible by both 4 and 6. Which of the following could it be?
questions.explanation: Given: A number divisible by both 4 and 6 must be divisible by their LCM. LCM of 4 and 6: 4 = 2²​ 6 = 2 × 3 LCM = 2²​× 3 = 12 The number must be divisible by 12. Now check the options: Option (a): 24 24 ÷ 12 = 2 (Divisible) Option (b): 18 18 ÷ 12 = 1.5 (Not divisible) Option (c): 20 20 ÷ 12 = 1.66 (Not divisible) Option (d): 30 30 ÷ 12 = 2.5 (Not divisible) So, only 24 is divisible by both 4 and 6. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: In the following number-pairs, the second number is obtained by applying certain mathematical operations to the first number. Select the pair in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following pairs. (Note: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g., 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.) 59, 71.6 86, 98.6
questions.explanation: Information Given: 59 → 71.6 86 → 98.6 Logic: Second number = First number + 12.6 Step-by-step: 59 + 12.6 = 71.6 ✔ 86 + 12.6 = 98.6 ✔ Check options: A: 77 + 12.6 = 89.6 ≠ 91.6 B: 92 + 12.6 = 104.6 ≠ 106.6 C: 41 + 12.6 = 53.6 ≠ 63.6 D: 65 + 12.6 = 77.6 ✔ Final Answer: 65, 77.6 Final Correct Option: D

questions.question: If 1 is added to each odd digit and 2 is subtracted from each even digit in the number 7435681, then what will be the sum of the digits that are third from the left and third from the right?
questions.explanation: Given: 7435681 Add 1 to each odd digit Subtract 2 from each even digit Now process each digit: 7 (odd) → 7 + 1 = 8 4 (even) → 4 − 2 = 2 3 (odd) → 3 + 1 = 4 5 (odd) → 5 + 1 = 6 6 (even) → 6 − 2 = 4 8 (even) → 8 − 2 = 6 1 (odd) → 1 + 1 = 2 New number: 8246462 Third from the left = 4 Third from the right = 4 Add them: 4 + 4 = 8 So, 8 will be the sum of the digits that are third from the left and third from the right. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: Statement: 1. All teachers are educated. 2. No uneducated person is respected. Conclusions: I. All teachers are respected. II. No educated person is uneducated. ​​
questions.explanation: Statement: 1. All teachers are educated. 2. No uneducated person is respected. From the given statements possible Venn diagram will be. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/609135aa-2fff-4bf0-8646-d89b5d17fdbe.png) Conclusions: I. All teachers are respected. (False, there is no relation between teachers and respected). II. No educated person is uneducated. (True, there is no direct or indirect relation between educated person and uneduacted). So, ​Only II follows. Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: In this question, a statement is given followed by two conclusions. Choose the conclusion(s) which best fit (s) logically. Statement: Video libraries are flourishing very much these days. Conclusions: I. People in general have got a video craze. II. It is much cheaper to see as many movies as one likes on videos rather than going to the cinema hall.
options: Neither conclusions I nor II follows
Either conclusion I or II follows
questions.explanation: In the statement, it is given that video libraries are flourishing very much these days but in the conclusion (I) it is given that people in general have got a video craze mean the people recently video craze which is incorrect. In the (II) conclusion it is given that it is much cheaper but in the statement, there is not mentioned about price of the video. Therefore, Neither conclusions I nor II follows

questions.question: ​In a code language, VEIL is written as 2592. What is the code for MEET?
questions.explanation: Given: ​ In a code language, VEIL is written as 2592. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/dd874d0e-6ca3-4a5f-bc24-b7e6be6122c9.png) Logic: Each letter is coded as 2nd digit of its positional value. For, VEIL - 2592 V = 22 - 2, E = 5, I = 9, L = 12 - 2 Similarly, MEET - ? M = 13 - 3, E = 5, E = 5, T = 20 - 0 So, MEET is written as 3550. Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: Select the correct mirror image of the given figure when a vertical mirror is placed to the right of the figure. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/bcc40838-95a0-4635-b470-16cea0ad8f11.png)
questions.explanation: The mirror image of the given figure as shown below. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/f8460e1a-f57a-4d2e-a356-1a32ec7999ec.png) Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which is the one that DOES NOT belong to that group? (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)
questions.explanation: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/ff1906ba-0eae-48d1-bd5e-ec7322710515.png) Logic: ​first letter +5 and second letter −5. A) Z(26)→E(5) = +5 ; S(19)→N(14) = −5 → (±5) B) E(5)→J(10) = +5 ; A(1)→V(22) = −5 → (±5) C) M(13)→R(18) = +5 ; Q(17)→K(11) = −6 → Not equal D) C(3)→H(8) = +5 ; O(15)→J(10) = −5 → (±5) Only option C does not follow ±5 pattern. Final Answer: MQ – RK Final Correct Option: C

questions.question: In a certain code language, ' A+B ' means ' A is the daughter of B ', ' A - B ' means ' A is the husband of B ', ' A×B ' means ' A is the wife of B ' and ' A÷B ' means ' A is the son of B '. How is P related to T if ' P+Q-R+S×T'?
questions.explanation: Given: ​​' A+B ' means ' A is the daughter of B ', Sign + - × ÷ Relations Daughter Husband Wife Son ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/ba2bbd02-f1de-4d5d-a0c5-c911f7a5144a.png) ' P+Q-R+S×T'? ​From the given information relationship diagram will be:​ ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/15c4405c-05bc-4df6-af0a-09ce3bdceb4f.png) Final Answer: Daughter’s daughter Final Correct Option: (c)

questions.question: Seven people, A, B, C, D, E, F and G, are sitting in a straight line, facing the north. A sits third from the left end of the line. Only one person sits between A and B. Only three people sit between C and B. E sits to the immediate right of C. D sits to the immediate right of G. How many people sit to the right of F?
questions.explanation: Given: Seven people, A, B, C, D, E, F and G, are sitting in a straight line, facing the north. A sits third from the left end of the line. Only one person sits between A and B. Only three people sit between C and B. E sits to the immediate right of C. D sits to the immediate right of G. From the given information seating arrangement will be. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/50c0ff3a-1b88-4f6c-83ef-28f91dd60707.png) So, three people sit to the right of F. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: Which of the following letter-number clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete? ACT123, IKB110, QSJ97, YAR84, ?
questions.explanation: Information Given: Series: ACT123, IKB110, QSJ97, YAR84, ? ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/4ac59d44-7719-490c-8026-e9b872380aa5.png) Logic: Letters: each position moves +8 alphabetically. Numbers: decrease by 13 each step. Step-by-step: 1st letters: A → I → Q → Y → G (+8 cyclic) 2nd letters: C → K → S → A → I (+8 cyclic) 3rd letters: T → B → J → R → Z (+8 cyclic) Numbers: 123 → 110 → 97 → 84 → 71 (−13 each) So next cluster = G I Z 71. Final Answer: GIZ71 Final Correct Option: C

questions.question: What is the angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at 3 o'clock?
questions.explanation: Information Given: Time: 3 o’clock Formula Used: Angle between hands = |30H − 5.5M| Logic: At exact 3:00, minute hand at 12, hour hand at 3. Each hour mark = 30°. Distance from 12 to 3 = 3 × 30 = 90°. Short Trick: At exact hour, angle = Hour × 30°. Final Answer: 90° Final Correct Option: (D)

questions.question: Each of L, M, N, O, W, X and Y has an exam on a different day of a week starting from Monday and ending on Sunday of the same week. Only Y has exam before X. Only three people have exam between M and X. Only three people have exam between L and O. W has exam on some day before O and on some day after N. How many people have exam after L?
questions.explanation: Given: ​Each of L, M, N, O, W, X and Y has an exam on a different day of a week starting from Monday and ending on Sunday of the same week. Only Y has exam before X. Only three people have exam between M and X. Only three people have exam between L and O. W has exam on some day before O and on some day after N. Day Person Monday Y Tuesday X Wednesday L Thursday N Friday W Saturday M Sunday O So, L has exam on Wednesday. After Wednesday: Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday Persons: N, W, M, O Total = 4 Thus, the correct option is: (d)

questions.question: Which of the following cubes in the answer figure cannot be made based on the unfolded cube in the question figure? ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/13c22165-5dbc-4eb9-b3b4-17b1e0f27f52.png) ​​
questions.explanation: Given: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/8de01385-a151-46c3-ac2c-d719088b3bd4.png) ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/1a280d87-480c-4e37-b02f-c93ff38a5712.png) In option (d) cube opposite elements are show, that means this cube cannot be made based on the unfolded cube in the question figure. Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: Ten people are sitting in two parallel rows 1 and 2. A, B, C, D, and E are sitting in row 1 facing north, and V, W, X, Y, and Z are sitting in row 2 facing south, in such a way that each member in row 1 faces a member of row 2. A is second from the right end of the row. W faces an immediate neighbour of A. X sits third to the right of W. The one who faces X, sits to the immediate right of B. Only one person sits between B and C. V sits to the immediate right of Y. D does not sit at any of the extreme ends of the row. Who faces Z?
questions.explanation: Given: Ten people are sitting in two parallel rows 1 and 2. A, B, C, D, and E are sitting in row 1 facing north, V, W, X, Y, and Z are sitting in row 2 facing south, A is second from the right end of the row. W faces an immediate neighbour of A. X sits third to the right of W. The one who faces X, sits to the immediate right of B. Only one person sits between B and C. V sits to the immediate right of Y. D does not sit at any of the extreme ends of the row. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/164147b3-c0a7-435d-a25b-a133a017b7f0.png) B faces Z. Thus, correct option is (d).

questions.question: Six boxes A, B, C, D, E and F are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. B is kept second from the bottom. Only one box is kept between B and F. Only three boxes are kept between C and D. D is kept immediately below B. Only two boxes are kept between A and E. A is kept immediately above C. Which box is third from the bottom?
questions.explanation: Given: ​Six boxes A, B, C, D, E and F are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. B is kept second from the bottom. Only one box is kept between B and F. Only three boxes are kept between C and D. D is kept immediately below B. Only two boxes are kept between A and E. A is kept immediately above C. Now final arrangement: Position (from Bottom) Box 1 (Bottom) D 2 B 3 E 4 F 5 C 6 (Top) A So, box E is third from the bottom. Final Correct Option: D

questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series based on the English alphabetical order? WIB UFX SCT QZP ?
questions.explanation: ​Information Given: Series: WIB, UFX, SCT, QZP, ?​ ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/678ad11c-3f37-4215-af4c-592f14f3ab52.png) Logic: First letters −2 , second letters −3 cyclic, third letters −4 in alphabetically. Step-by-step: 1st letters: W → U → S → Q → O (−2 each) 2nd letters: I → F → C → Z → W (−3 each) 3rd letters: B → X → T → P → L (−4 each) Next term = O W L. Final Answer: OWL Final Correct Option: C

questions.question: Rohan starts from Point A and drives 4 km towards East. He then takes a right turn, drives 3 km, turns left and drives 6 km. He then takes a left turn and drives 3 km. He takes a final right turn, drives 3 km and stops at Point P. How far (shortest distance) and towards which direction should he drive in order to reach Point A again? (All turns are 90 degrees turns only unless specified)
questions.explanation: Given: ​Rohan starts from Point A and drives 4 km towards East. He then takes a right turn, drives 3 km, turns left and drives 6 km. He then takes a left turn and drives 3 km. He takes a final right turn, drives 3 km and stops at Point P. From the given information path diagram will be: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/2cc80ee9-9241-48f3-976b-dd0d427775db.png) So, he drive 13 km towards West to reach Point A again. Final Correct Option: D

questions.question: Refer to the following series and answer the question that follows (all numbers are single digit numbers only). Counting to be done from left to right only. (Left) 1 8 9 1 3 8 3 8 9 9 5 9 6 5 5 1 7 7 4 2 3 1 6 (Right) How many such even digits are there each of which is immediately preceded by an even digit and also immediately followed by an even digit?
questions.explanation: Given: (Left) 1 8 9 1 3 8 3 8 9 9 5 9 6 5 5 1 7 7 4 2 3 1 6 (Right) Logic: Even digit | Even digit | Even digit So, none such even digits are there each of which is immediately preceded by an even digit and also immediately followed by an even digit. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: In the following triads, each group of letters is related to the subsequent one following a certain logic. Select from the given options, the one that follows the same logic. PAST - PSTA - ASPT QUAD - QADU - UAQD
questions.explanation: Given: PAST - PSTA - ASPT QUAD - QADU - UAQD Logic: Position of letters are interchanged. For, PAST - PSTA - ASPT ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/b93181f4-3715-44a9-8836-c37930737f30.png) For, QUAD - QADU - UAQD ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/a79e475e-04c6-4e01-ab65-76be57f36919.png) Similarly, IDEA - IEAD - DEIA ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/eb0c1e63-2bda-49e7-a427-bd4075f52776.png) Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: Which mathematical signs should come in place of A and B to make the equation correct? 18 A 6 B 3 = 36
questions.explanation:
Given: 18 A 6 B 3 = 36 Using BODMAS rule. ​Operation preference wiseSymbolBrackets[],,()Orders, of(power),√(root),ofDivision÷Multiplication×Addition+Subtraction− egin{array}{|c|c|} \hline	extbf{Operation preference wise} & 	extbf{Symbol} \\hline	ext{Brackets} &[],{}, () \ \hline 	ext{Orders, of} & (power), √ (root) , of \ \hline 	ext{Division}& ÷ \ \hline 	ext{Multiplication} & × \ \hline 	ext{Addition} & + \ \hline 	ext{Subtraction} & - \\hline\end{array}Operation preference wiseBracketsOrders, ofDivisionMultiplicationAdditionSubtraction​Symbol[],,()(power),√(root),of÷×+−​​ Now, we check each options. ​Option (a): ​+ and × New equation: 18 + 6 × 3 = 36 18 + 18 = 36 36 = 36 ​Option (b): × and - ​New equation: 18 × 6 - 3 = 36 108 - 3 = 36 105 ≠
e= 36​ ​Option (c): ​- and × ​New equation: 18 - 6 × 3 = 36 18 - 18 = 36 0 ≠
e= 36​ ​Option (d): ​+ and - ​New equation: 18 + 6 - 3 = 36 24 - 3 = 36 21 ≠
e= 36​ Thus, correct option is (a).
